name: Stack
summary: Flexbox container that lays children out in one `direction` (column/row) with consistent `gap`, token-aware `align`/`justify`, per-axis `gapX`/`gapY`, a `divider`, and responsive (breakpoint-map) props that compile to CSS @media — no <Surface> needed. The default choice for one-dimensional flow; use `Grid` for two-dimensional layouts. Sugar: `HStack`, `VStack`, `Center`, `Cluster`, `Spacer`.

props.name: wrap
props.description:
Wrap children when they overflow the main axis. Default: `true` for rows,
`false` for columns. Scalar (not responsive).

props.name: gap
props.description: Inter-child gap as units or CSS length. Default 1 unit. Responsive.

props.name: pad
props.description: Container padding as units or CSS length. Default 1 unit. Responsive.

props.name: align
props.description:
Cross-axis item alignment (`align-items`). Defaults to the direction's
natural alignment: row → `center`, column → `stretch`. Responsive.

props.name: justify
props.description: Main-axis distribution (`justify-content`). Unset by default. Responsive.

props.name: alignX
props.description:
Horizontal placement of the whole stack when it is not full width
(auto-margin anchor — distinct from `align`, which positions children).

props.name: divider
props.description:
Node interleaved BETWEEN children (e.g. a `<Divider/>`). For a row stack,
pass a vertically-oriented divider.

props.name: scroll
props.description: Overflow behavior. `'auto-y'` (default) keeps the legacy vertical-scroll.

props.name: grow
props.description: Grow factor when the stack is itself a flex child. `true` → 1.

props.name: data-valet-component
props.description: Override the machine-readable component marker (sugar/composites self-identify).

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: compact
props.description:
Zeros all layout spacing (pad, gap, and spacing-margins) where supported and
cascades to spacing-aware descendants. Does not touch control insets,
structural geometry, border-radius, glyph sizes, or alignment. `compact={false}`
opts a subtree back out of an inherited compact.

props.name: density
props.description: Density override for the spacing scale; defaults from nearest Surface.

props.name: as
props.description: Polymorphic element override (renders this element/component instead of the default).

bestPractices: Use for one-dimensional layouts (rows/columns) with consistent gaps; reach for `Grid` for two-dimensional layouts and `Table` for data.
Prefer `align`/`justify` tokens (start/center/end/stretch/baseline · start/center/end/between/around/evenly) over `sx={{ alignItems, justifyContent }}` — they are typed and density-aware.

Make any of `direction`/`gap`/`pad`/`align`/`justify`/`gapX`/`gapY` responsive by passing a breakpoint map (`{ xs, sm, md, lg, xl }`); it compiles to CSS @media in the styled rule, so it is correct on the server's first paint and needs no <Surface>. Keep maps to enumerable values — don't bind one to continuously-varying state.
Keep spacing tokenized (numeric `gap`/`pad` units) so a `compact` or `density` ancestor rescales the whole subtree; avoid fixed pixel gaps.

Reach for the sugar when it reads better: `HStack`/`VStack` for directional rows/columns (HStack centers cross-axis by default), `Center` to center on both axes, `Cluster` for a wrapping row of chips/tags/buttons, and `<Spacer/>` inside a row to push siblings apart.
Use `divider` to interleave a separator between children (pass a vertically-oriented `<Divider/>` for a row); children must be flat (a Fragment counts as one node).

examples.title: Responsive direction (compiles to @media, no Surface)
examples.code:
<Stack direction={{ xs: 'column', md: 'row' }} gap={{ xs: 1, md: 2 }} align='center'>
  <Box>Sidebar</Box>
  <Box fullWidth>Main</Box>
</Stack>
